title: Consistent Hashing Explained
summary: A distributed system hashing technique that minimizes re-distribution of keys when the hash table size changes. It maps each input to a fixed point on an n-dimensional space.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Consistent hashing is used in distributed systems, especially for caching and data storage to evenly distribute keys among nodes while minimizing re-distribution when the number of servers changes.

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: In consistent hashing, both input and output values are treated as points on a ring or circle. Each node in the distributed system is assigned a random number within this range corresponding to its identifier.
key_points: Mapping inputs and outputs onto an n-dimensional space

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: It provides consistent performance during scale operations, ensuring minimal disruption when nodes are added or removed from the system.
key_points: Minimizes rehashes and redistributions of keys

key_facts.one key fact: Consistent hashing helps to maintain consistent performance during scale operations, ensuring minimal disruption when nodes are added or removed from the system.
